# GlassKit UI

> The open-source React component library for Meta Ray-Ban Display (smart-glasses) web apps. It ships the platform primitives Meta does not: a glasses-tuned D-pad focus engine, Neural Band gesture hooks, and a full set of premium HUD components for the 600×600 lens.

Design language (2026-06, "premium surfaces"): a **calm dark base** (`#0b0e16`→`#090b11`), **surfaces that pop** (top-lit gradient fill + 1px light edge + soft depth), and **tasteful gradient icon plates** (iOS/Meta app-icon style). One refined blue accent (`--accent: #4c8dff`), white-dominant ink, HUD-scale type, and tight glasses spacing (≈8–22px margins, ~88px touch targets, fill the screen — not a shrunk webpage). Rules: one task per view, glanceable, tabular numerals, RTL-safe logical CSS, never mirror world-anchored components, motion is ease-out + reduced-motion-safe. See `docs/design/meta-hud-language.md`.

## SDK (`@glasskit/glasses-ui`)

- `GlassViewport` — the 600×600 lens surface; wrap your app in it.
- `useDpad()` — arrow keys / Neural Band move a focus ring between `.focusable` elements; Enter activates.
- `useDeviceOrientation()`, `useDeviceMotion()`, `useGeolocation()`, `useNeuralBand()` — W3C sensor + gesture hooks.
- `styles.css` — the premium-surfaces design system, scoped under `.glass-viewport`.

## Components

Vendored shadcn-style (you own the source). Each has a live preview, props table, and usage at `/docs/components/<slug>`.

- **Shell** — Screen (status / stage / cue layout).
- **Display** — Readout, Cue, GlowIcon (line glyph or gradient plate), Heading, Badge, Meter (ring gauge), StatGrid (surface stat cards), Progress (bar / step dots).
- **Action** — Button, List + ListRow (fills + scroll-progress), Toggle, Stepper, Segmented, Confirm, QuickReplyChips.
- **Status / feedback** — AsyncView, StatusDot, ErrorState, Toast (static surface), **Toaster** (the dynamic toast/notification system — see below).
- **Navigation** — Launcher (gradient app grid), Deck (paged flow).
- **Spatial (world-anchored, never mirrored)** — DirectionArrow, Compass, Reticle, Pin, Callout.
- **Comms** — Avatar (initials / photo), NotificationCard (avatar + sender + preview + actions).
- **Media** — NowPlaying (art + scrubber + times).
- **AI** — AssistantOrb (Meta-AI presence; idle / listening / thinking / speaking).

## Toasts & notifications

`<Toaster>` + `toast()` is the dynamic system, built on **Sonner** (themed to the lens). Mount `<Toaster>` once at your app root; it is **bottom-anchored** (below the sightline, like the real Display) and handles the queue, stacking, auto-dismiss, and enter/exit motion.

```tsx
<Toaster />                                  // once, at the root
toast("Workout saved");
toast("Mara Lin", { description: "On my way", icon: <Avatar name="Mara Lin" /> });
toast.custom((id) => <NotificationCard … />);
```

`NotificationCard` is the inline/static notification surface; `Toast` is the static one-liner surface; `Toaster`/`toast()` is the live system that shows them.

## Links

- Docs / getting started: https://glasskit.app/ui/docs
- Components reference: https://glasskit.app/ui/docs/components
- Playground (live customizer): https://glasskit.app/ui/playground
- Source: https://github.com/GlassKitApp/glasskit-ui

## Notes

- React 19 is a peer dependency. ESM only.
- The repo is not yet public and nothing is published to npm — these are the intended URLs.
